cleavage agent: we mainly use trypsin for digestion purposes.
collision energy: with a normalized collision energy of 27.
instrument: emitted into a Thermo Q-Exactive HF tandem mass spectrometer.
ionization type: The Thermo Q-Exactive HF was set to positive mode.
labeling: peptides were separated with a Waters NanoAcquity UPLC and emitted into a Thermo Q-Exactive HF tandem mass spectrometer.
